pr.title: use enum item ID in ftMewtwo_SpecialN, move more ASM struct definitions to C and fix typo in ClampFallSpeed function name
pr.author: VetriTheRetri

summary: Small fighter cleanup PR that moved Mario/Luigi SpecialLw collision-box data out of ASM labels and into typed C statics, added a shared ftCollisionBox typedef, corrected the ftComm_ClampFallSpeed spelling, and replaced Mewtwo Shadow Ball's raw item kind literal with the It_Kind_Mewtwo_ShadowBall enum. The obj_files.mk ordering was adjusted so the remaining asm object still links before the C object after removing data labels from assembly.
what_changed: Removed Mario's lbl_803C72A0 collision-box data from asm/melee/ft/chara/ftmario.s and replaced its C uses with static ftMario_SpecialLw_CollisionBox in ftMario_SpecialLw.c.
Removed Luigi's lbl_803D0AE8 collision-box data from asm/melee/ft/chara/ftluigi.s and replaced its C uses with static ftLuigi_SpecialLw_CollisionBox in ftLuigi_SpecialLw.c.
Added ftCollisionBox to fighter.h with fields top, bottom, left, and right, matching the six-f32 collision-box data previously represented by anonymous asm labels or a local UnkEnvCollBox struct.
Changed func_800824A0 and func_80082888 extern declarations in Luigi SpecialLw from struct UnkEnvCollBox* to ftCollisionBox*.
Renamed ftComm_ClampFalllSpeed to ftComm_ClampFallSpeed in ftcommon.c, ftcommon.h, and Mario/Luigi SpecialLw callers.
Changed ftMewtwo_SpecialN_LaunchShadowBall to pass It_Kind_Mewtwo_ShadowBall instead of the raw literal 0x70 to func_802C519C.
Adjusted obj_files.mk so ftmario.s.o and ftluigi.s.o are listed before their corresponding C object files, preserving link/data ordering after data moved from asm into C.
smart_moves: Converted opaque assembly data labels into file-local static C objects with descriptive names, improving readability while keeping the same numeric initializer values: { 12.0F, 0.0F, -6.0F, 6.0F, 6.0F, 6.0F }.
Introduced a reusable ftCollisionBox typedef in fighter.h instead of leaving per-file unknown structs, allowing collision helper prototypes to become more semantically accurate.
Used the existing item enum for Mewtwo's Shadow Ball item kind, avoiding a magic number at a callsite that already had item-kind semantics.
Fixed the triple-l typo in ftComm_ClampFalllSpeed consistently across declaration, definition, and known callsites.
Handled object ordering in obj_files.mk alongside the asm-to-C data move, which is important for matching and link layout in decomp projects.
decomp_lessons: When an asm label is just structured constant data, migrate it into a typed static C object once the owning source file can emit matching data.
After moving data between asm and C, check obj_files.mk ordering; object order may need to change to preserve binary layout or symbol placement.
Prefer project enums such as It_Kind_Mewtwo_ShadowBall over raw item-kind literals like 0x70 when the enum identity is known.
Small spelling fixes to common function names should be applied atomically to headers, definitions, and all callers to avoid drift.
Replace temporary unknown structs only when the field layout and use sites provide enough evidence for a stable shared type.
naming_conventions: Correct common fighter utility spelling from ftComm_ClampFalllSpeed to ftComm_ClampFallSpeed.
Name file-local collision box statics after the owning character/action, e.g. ftMario_SpecialLw_CollisionBox and ftLuigi_SpecialLw_CollisionBox.
Use ft-prefixed shared fighter data types in fighter.h, e.g. ftCollisionBox.
Use item-kind enum identifiers such as It_Kind_Mewtwo_ShadowBall instead of numeric literals in item-spawn calls.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Removed six-f32 collision-box constants from character asm files and reintroduced them as static C data in the corresponding SpecialLw source files.
Kept exact float values from the asm data: top 12.0F, bottom 0.0F, left x/y -6.0F/6.0F, right x/y 6.0F/6.0F.
Changed object file order for Mario and Luigi so asm/melee/ft/chara/ftmario.s.o and asm/melee/ft/chara/ftluigi.s.o are emitted before the character C objects after the data relocation.
Used BOOL/FALSE/TRUE in rewritten collision branches where the original code compared against integer 0/1, aligning with the helper return types without changing branch meaning.
review_feedback: No issue comments or review comments were present in the provided PR slice. The only evidence of review is one review record with no excerpted feedback.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that further type func_800824A0 or func_80082888 and confirm ftCollisionBox semantics.
Search for remaining ftComm_ClampFalllSpeed typo references or other misspelled ftComm utility names.
Search for remaining raw item-kind literals in fighter item-spawn calls that can be replaced with It_Kind_* enums.
Find other asm labels containing small float collision/ECB structures that can be moved into typed C statics.
